name: action-realization-probe
spec_doc_section: PR-4 — action-realization-probe (Life-Harness narrow slice, PROBE ONLY)
mechanism_described_but_NOT_implemented: pre-execution Action Realization Layer wrapping the tool registry — deterministic validation/canonicalization/blocking of malformed tool calls before they execute. v0.9 ships ONLY this probe, never the mechanism. If the probe ACCEPTs, v0.10 may build the mechanism; otherwise the rejection joins the five other evaluated rejections.
binding_thesis: An action-realization layer that blocks/canonicalizes malformed tool calls BEFORE execution provides a measurable lift over KAOS v0.8.3 native (critical-step localizer + diagnoser + retry-with-feedback) on the action-class failure slice, with causal isolation, no regression on the non-action control slice, and inline overhead within the two-timescale budget.

workload_invariants.source: organic — sampled from existing dream_runs / failure_fingerprints / critical_steps populated by v0.8.3 native classification

action_class_slice.definition: tool-call failures where the v0.8.3 critical-step localizer (or its surrogate) tags the failure as action-class (malformed args, schema violation, unparseable tool input, unknown-tool)
action_class_slice.label_source: failure_fingerprints.taxonomy_class = 'action' OR critical_steps with rationale matching action-class regex

control_slice.definition: tool-call failures where v0.8.3 classifies non-action (planning, memory, system)

workload_invariants.synthetic_fallback: NONE — synthetic workload IS NOT permitted under this lock. If organic n_action < 200 OR n_control < 200, the harness MUST emit VOID#1 (insufficient organic sample). No retune-and-rerun under VOID#1 is permitted to substitute synthetic data; the next attempt requires more organic data, not a different sampler.

arms.B0: naive baseline — execute every tool call without any validation, diagnoser, or retry. Establishes the worst-case bar; the probe MUST beat B0 by a large margin to be sane.
arms.B1: v0.8.3 native baseline — critical-step localizer + diagnoser + retry-with-feedback. THE real bar.
arms.FULL: Action Realization Layer — pre-execution validation/canonicalization/blocking of malformed tool calls before the tool boundary. (Probe-only: the layer is implemented as a thin wrapper used ONLY inside this bench; it does NOT ship in kaos/.)
arms.L1: lesion — Action Realization Layer registered but should_activate=False. Isolates 'library presence' from 'intervention'.
arms.L2: lesion — Action Realization Layer fires on a random subset (same activation rate as FULL, but uncorrelated with failure prediction). Controls for 'any intervention helps'.

G1.name: beats v0.8.3 native baseline
G1.predicate: FULL completion-rate on action-class slice MINUS B1 completion-rate on action-class slice >= 0.040, bootstrap95 CI lower bound > 0.0, n >= 200
G1.rationale: the real bar — must beat KAOS-v0.8.3-native, not naive. Anything less than +4pp on the slice the mechanism is designed for is indistinguishable from the localizer-plus-diagnoser KAOS already has.

G2.name: causal isolation via lesions
G2.rationale: lift must come from the validation intervention, not from library presence (L1) or arbitrary intervention (L2).

G3.name: no regression on non-action control slice
G3.predicate: FULL completion-rate on control slice >= B1 completion-rate on control slice - 0.010
G3.rationale: explicit guard against the SWE-Skills-Bench failure mode (3/49 deployed skills DEGRADED performance). A feature that helps where it should but quietly hurts elsewhere is a net loss.

G4.name: inline overhead within two-timescale budget
G4.predicate: p95 inline overhead per tool call < 500 microseconds
G4.rationale: KAOS's inline hooks must stay in microsecond territory. Anything slower belongs in the dream-cycle, not the call path.

G0.name: sanity floor
G0.predicate: EVERY arm achieves >= 0.95 completion-rate on a frozen sanity slice of trivially-valid tool calls (well-formed args, known tool, no schema violation). Catches harness defects where an arm refuses to execute anything.
G0.rationale: VOID-on-fail. If an arm can't even execute trivially-valid calls, the run is uninterpretable.

verdict_rule: VOID if G0 fails or judge-audit kappa < 0.85 or workload n minima unmet. ACCEPT iff G1 AND G2 AND G3 AND G4 all pass. ANY single kill-gate failure REJECTS. No retune-and-rerun.
no_retune_clause: Thresholds in kill_gates are BYTE-FROZEN at this commit. A change to any threshold or predicate requires a NEW commit + a NEW entry in KNOWN_LOCK_SHA256. Goalpost moves are mechanically impossible: the harness refuses to run on any lock whose sha256 is not pre-registered.

judge.implementation: kaos.eval.harness.judge.judge_arm with SurrogateVerifier in heuristic mode (router=None)
judge.label_source: mechanical: did the tool call ultimately produce a non-error result within the arm's allowed retry budget? Set-membership over canonical outcomes; the verifier never sees arm identity.

honest_expected_outcome: REJECT or VOID is the likely outcome (~0.55 confidence per the SWE-Skills-Bench negative prior and the strong v0.8.3 native baseline). REJECT is a SUCCESSFUL outcome under this discipline — it joins SAGE, synthesis-as-consolidation, AutoResearchClaw, HASP as evaluated-and-rejected, freeing v0.10 to ship something with real evidence rather than a mechanism we hope works.
